front: Play: hitmen Ben and Gus argue about 'light the kettle'; receive restaurant orders through the title device; Ben is hired to kill Gus
Play: Goldberg and McCann terrorize Stanley at Meg's boarding house; blind man's bluff game; Meg gives Stanley a toy drum
Play: told in reverse chronological order; Jerry's affair with his friend Robert's wife Emma
Play: Ruth stays to work as a prostitute while Teddy returns to America; Joey doesn't 'go the whole hog'
Play: Mick smashes a Buddha statue; homeless Davies never goes to Sidcup to recover his papers
Playwright: 'comedies of menace' term coined by Irving Wardle; Nobel lecture 'Art, Truth & Politics' denounced Iraq invasion
